repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.game: fixed-timestep game loop, entity-component-system, collision detection and physics, per-frame budget, asset pipeline, and deterministic simulation for netcode
write a nightly batch ETL job that aggregates analytics into a warehouse

retrieval_query: game loop fixed timestep entity component system physics collision frame budget asset pipeline input determinism netcode interpolation

validation_smoke.name: code.game L3 validation smoke
signals: simulation step is fixed-timestep and frame-rate independent
frame time stays within budget at p99 (no dropped frames)
physics result is deterministic across runs given the same inputs
no per-frame heap allocation in the hot loop (no GC hitch)
input is sampled and applied within the same simulation tick

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.game diagnosis smoke
signals: movement speed tied to frame rate (variable timestep)
frame stalls from garbage-collection or asset loading on the main thread
non-deterministic physics breaking replay or netcode
collision check is O(n^2) over all entities
input read at render time, causing one-frame lag or missed inputs
